oligos.name: Anchored barcoded oligo-dT RT primer
oligos.role: Reverse-transcription primer that introduces the first-round (RT) cell barcode and the UMI while priming on the mRNA poly(A) tail

components.name: oligo(dT)30 + VN anchor
components.role: 30-mer poly-T with a 2-nt anchored VN 3' end (V = A/C/G, N = any base)

oligos.notes: Documented as 5'-ACGACGCTCTTCCGATCTNNNNNNNN[10bp index]TTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTVN-3' (IDT). Used at 25 uM. Same primer design used for the related bulk Tn5-RNA-seq control.

oligos.name: P5 PCR primer (indexed, i5)
oligos.role: Library PCR primer that adds the Illumina P5 adaptor and the i5 (second-round) index; anneals to the RT-derived R1 handle

components.name: Read1 primer region
components.role: TruSeq Read1 primer binding site; 3' end overlaps the RT primer R1 handle

oligos.notes: Documented as 5'-AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC[i5]ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT-3' (IDT), 10 uM. In three-level indexing an 'indexed P5 primer' of the same structure is used.

oligos.name: P7 PCR primer (indexed, i7)
oligos.role: Library PCR primer that adds the Illumina P7 adaptor and the i7 (second-round) index; anneals to the Tn5 Nextera adaptor

components.name: Nextera S7 region
components.role: Anneals to the Tn5 (Nextera) transposon adaptor introduced by tagmentation

oligos.name: Tn5 (Nextera) mosaic-end transposon adaptor
oligos.role: Transposase-loaded adaptor inserted at the 3' cDNA end during tagmentation; provides the Read2 / i7-side handle

components.name: Transferred strand (Nextera S7 + mosaic end)
components.role: Strand transferred to DNA; 3' 19-mer is the mosaic end (AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG)

components.name: Non-transferred / mosaic-end complement
components.role: 19-nt reverse complement of the mosaic end

oligos.notes: Provided pre-loaded in the Nextera DNA Sample Preparation kit / TDE1 (Illumina). Sequence is the standard Nextera mosaic end; only the i7-side adaptor is retained in the final library because the PCR primers are RT-product-specific. In three-level indexing a barcoded (indexed) TDE1/Tn5 introduces a third cellular index at this junction.

oligos.name: Read 1 sequencing primer (TruSeq Read1)
oligos.role: Primes Read 1 to sequence the UMI then the RT barcode
oligos.notes: Standard Illumina TruSeq Read1 primer; matches the R1 region built by the RT primer + P5 primer.

oligos.name: Read 2 sequencing primer (Nextera Read2)
oligos.role: Primes Read 2 to sequence the cDNA 3' tag from the Tn5 mosaic-end side
oligos.notes: Standard Nextera Read2 primer (S7 + mosaic end).

final_library.source_label: sci-RNA-seq final Illumina library (two-level indexing), Cao et al. Science 2017, Fig. 1B + Methods
annotation_lines: ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT = Read 1 primer region (TruSeq R1 / RT handle)
[UMI:8] = 8-nt unique molecular identifier (sequenced first in Read 1)
[CELL_BARCODE:10] = RT barcode (first-round cellular index; sequenced next in Read 1)
TTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTT = oligo(dT)30 (poly-T from RT primer / poly-A junction)
CCGAGCCCACGAGAC = Nextera S7 / Read 2 primer region (reverse complement)
[SAMPLE_INDEX:10] = i7 index (reverse complement; second-round PCR barcode)

evidence.locator: sci-RNA-seq final Illumina library (two-level indexing), Cao et al. Science 2017, Fig. 1B + Methods

library_generation.title: Fix and distribute cells
library_generation.summary: Methanol-fixed (or lysed-to-nuclei) cells are distributed across 96-/384-well plates.
library_generation.note: 5M cells fixed in ice-cold 100% methanol, washed with RNase-inhibitor buffers, and 1,000-10,000 cells (optimally ~2,000 cells or ~5,000 nuclei) dispensed per well. Wells define the first-round sample/barcode assignment.

library_generation.title: In situ barcoded reverse transcription (1st index + UMI)
library_generation.summary: A well-specific barcoded oligo-dT primer with a UMI primes RT on the poly(A) tail in situ.
library_generation.note: 1 uL 25 uM anchored oligo-dT RT primer + dNTPs, denature 55C, then SuperScript IV mix; RT 55C 10 min. Each well delivers a distinct RT barcode; the UMI tags individual molecules.

library_generation.title: Pool and FACS-sort
library_generation.summary: All wells are pooled, DAPI-stained, and single cells/nuclei are FACS-sorted into a fresh plate.
library_generation.note: Gating on DAPI discriminates singlets from doublets; sorting into new wells sets up the second-round (PCR) barcode. Typically 10-100+ cells sorted per destination well.
library_generation.product: sorted single cell/nucleus in new well (barcoded mRNA:cDNA hybrid, as step 2)

library_generation.title: Second-strand synthesis
library_generation.summary: mRNA Second Strand Synthesis converts the RT product into double-stranded cDNA.
library_generation.note: NEB mRNA Second Strand Synthesis buffer + enzyme, 16C 150 min, stop 75C 20 min.

library_generation.title: Tn5 tagmentation (Nextera)
library_generation.summary: Tn5 transposase fragments the ds-cDNA and inserts Nextera mosaic-end adaptors.
library_generation.note: Nextera TD buffer + TDE1, 55C 5 min, with carrier genomic DNA to avoid over-tagmentation. In the three-level variant a barcoded/indexed Tn5 adds a third cellular index here. Only the i7-side adaptor is retained because downstream PCR primers are RT-product-specific.

library_generation.title: PCR with indexed P5/P7 (2nd index)
library_generation.summary: P5(i5) and P7(i7) primers amplify RT products and add flow-cell adaptors + the second index.
library_generation.note: 2 uL each 10 uM P5/P7 primer + NEBNext 2X master mix; 72C 5 min, 98C 30 s, 18-22 cycles (98C 10 s / 66C 30 s / 72C 1 min), 72C 5 min. RT barcode + PCR (i5/i7) barcode together form the full cellular index.

library_generation.title: Pool, purify, sequence
library_generation.summary: Amplicons are pooled, AMPure-purified, quantified, and sequenced on Illumina NextSeq 500.
library_generation.note: 0.8X AMPure cleanup; QC by Qubit and 6% TBE-PAGE. Sequenced with a V2 75-cycle kit (R1 18, R2 52, I1 10, I2 10).

library_sequencing.note: Read 1 primer anneals to the RT/R1 handle on the bottom strand and reads the 8-nt UMI immediately followed by the 10-nt RT barcode (8 + 10 = 18 cycles).
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 1 primer:
5'-ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT----------------------->
                                    (reads UMI 8 + RT barcode 10 = 18 nt)

5'-...ACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT[UMI:8][CELL_BARCODE:10]TTTTTTTTTT...[cDNA]-3'   (top strand)
3'-...TGTGCTGCGAGAAGGCTAGA[UMI:8][CELL_BARCODE:10]AAAAAAAAAA...[cDNA]-5'   (bottom strand)

library_sequencing.note: Read 2 primer anneals to the Tn5 mosaic-end/S7 region on the top strand and reads the 3' cDNA tag off the bottom strand (52 cycles).
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 2 primer:
                        3'-GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG-5'
<---------------------(reads cDNA 3' tag, 52 nt)

5'-...[cDNA]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC[i7:10]ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG-3'   (top strand)
3'-...[cDNA]GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG[i7:10]TAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC-5'   (bottom strand)

library_sequencing.note: The i7 index read primer anneals at the mosaic-end/S7 junction and reads the 10-nt i7 (second-round PCR) barcode toward P7.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Index 1 (i7) primer:
5'-GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG--->
                                        (reads i7, 10 nt)

5'-...CTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC[SAMPLE_INDEX:10(i7)]ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG-3'   (top strand)
3'-...GAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG[SAMPLE_INDEX:10(i7)]TAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC-5'   (bottom strand)

library_sequencing.note: On the NextSeq 500 the i5 (second-round PCR) barcode is read from the R1-handle primer toward P5 (10 cycles); on this patterned-flow-cell workflow it is read off the resynthesized bottom strand.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Index 2 (i5) primer:
              <---3'-TCTAGCCTTCTCGCAGCACATATCCCTTTCTCACA-5'
(reads i5, 10 nt)

5'-AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC[SAMPLE_INDEX:10(i5)]ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT...-3'   (top strand)
3'-TTACTATGCCGCTGGTGGCTCTAGATGTG[SAMPLE_INDEX:10(i5)]TGTGAGAAAGGGATGTGCTGCGAGAAGGCTAGA...-5'   (bottom strand)

title: sci-RNA-seq
description: Single-cell combinatorial indexing RNA sequencing (sci-RNA-seq): a plate-based, split-pool method that produces 3'-tag digital gene-expression profiles from tens of thousands of single cells or nuclei without physical isolation of each cell. Methanol-fixed cells are distributed to wells and given an in situ reverse-transcription with a well-specific barcoded oligo-dT primer carrying a UMI (first index); cells are pooled, FACS-sorted into a new plate, then second-strand synthesis, Tn5 (Nextera) tagmentation, and PCR with indexed P5/P7 primers add a second well-specific index. Read 1 captures the UMI + RT barcode, Read 2 captures the 3' end of the transcript, and the two index reads capture i5/i7; the combination of RT barcode + PCR barcode forms the cellular index.

original_publication.title: Comprehensive single-cell transcriptional profiling of a multicellular organism

throughput.summary: Two-level indexing routinely profiles ~10^4 single cells per experiment; demonstrated 576 x 960 indexing yielding ~4 x 10^4 cells; ~50,000 C. elegans cells profiled across two experiments. Three-level indexing projected to enable >10^6 cells.
throughput.cells: 42,035 C. elegans single-cell transcriptomes (experiment 1) + 7,325 (experiment 2, incl. 511 intestine); 15,997 cells in the 384x384 human/mouse demonstration
throughput.rna: 3'-tag digital gene expression; e.g. ~24,454 UMIs / 5,604 genes per human cell and ~17,665 UMIs / 4,065 genes per mouse cell (optimized 96x96); median 575 protein-coding-gene UMIs per C. elegans cell
throughput.dna: Not applicable (RNA assay); tagmented carrier genomic DNA is not amplified or sequenced because PCR primers are RT-product-specific

publication.statistical_model: Negative binomial (per-cell-type gene expression modeled as a negative binomial with mean mu and dispersion alpha; 95% CIs via normal approximation N(mu, (mu + mu^2*alpha)/n)); species collision rate modeled combinatorially

other.label: Cost per cell
other.value: $0.03 to $0.20 per cell (as low as <$0.025 with 1,536 RT barcodes)

other.label: Sequencer / kit
other.value: Illumina NextSeq 500, V2 75-cycle kit (R1 18, R2 52, I1 10, I2 10)

other.label: Transposase
other.value: Tn5 via Nextera DNA Sample Preparation kit (TDE1); tagmented carrier genomic DNA is not amplified because PCR primers are RT-product-specific

other.label: Data availability
other.value: GEO accession GSE98561; cell atlas at http://atlas.gs.washington.edu

other.label: Organism
other.value: Caenorhabditis elegans L2 larva (strain RW12139) plus HEK293T/HeLa S3/NIH-3T3 controls

data_processing.summary: Base calls are demultiplexed with bcl2fastq, adaptor-trimmed with trim_galore, and aligned to the reference genome with STAR; PCR duplicates are collapsed using the UMI together with the RT index and the read-2 tagmentation coordinate, reads are split into single-cell indices by the RT barcode (plus the Tn5 barcode for three-level indexing), and strand-specific exonic+intronic UMIs are counted per gene with HTSeq to build a digital gene-expression matrix that is reduced by PCA then t-SNE and clustered by the density-peak algorithm in Monocle.
